notes: These tests are based on the uploaded Customs Valuation I PDF.
The sequence follows a progression from foundational recall to broader application and mixed-topic reasoning.
Time limits increase toward later tests.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid consecutive repetition of the same answer letter.

tests.title: Customs Valuation I - Main Test 1
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Customs Valuation I notes.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one type of legal deduction that may be excluded if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable.

options.A: Assists supplied by the buyer
options.B: Charges for construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance undertaken after importation
options.C: Containers and packing
options.D: Commissions to the seller’s agent

questions.explanation: Post-importation service charges may be excluded if properly distinguished.

questions.question: Can duties and taxes of the importing country be excluded from customs value if distinguished from the price?

options.A: Yes
options.B: Only if the seller is unrelated
options.C: No
options.D: Only if transport is by air

questions.explanation: Import-country duties and taxes may be excluded if separated.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Can customs reject transaction value just because the price is lower than prevailing market prices?

options.A: Yes, if customs prefers a higher value
options.B: Only when goods are imported by road
options.C: No
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: A low price alone is not enough.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can the buyer’s own marketing activities form part of customs value?

options.A: Yes, always
options.B: Yes, if done after importation
options.C: Only if a buying commission is paid
options.D: Normally no, if undertaken on the buyer’s own account and not as a condition of sale

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities may fall outside customs value if not a condition of sale.

questions.question: Which document is especially important for establishing transaction value?

options.A: The warehouse ledger
options.B: The commercial invoice
options.C: The inland delivery note only
options.D: The employee contract

questions.explanation: The commercial invoice is central to transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why is greater uniformity and certainty important in customs valuation?

options.A: Because every importer must pay the same amount
options.B: Because valuation should vary by port
options.C: Because traders should know the rules in advance and trust that valuation will be applied consistently and fairly
options.D: Because customs officers should have unlimited discretion

questions.explanation: Uniformity and certainty support predictable trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is meant by proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use under Article 8.1(d)?

options.A: Any domestic resale profit kept entirely by the importer
options.B: Any customs penalty imposed after clearance
options.C: Any freight refund from the carrier
options.D: A payment or value from later resale, disposal, or use of the imported goods that accrues directly or indirectly to the seller

questions.explanation: Proceeds must accrue to the seller.

questions.question: When can royalties and licence fees be added to customs value?

options.A: When they relate to the goods being valued, are paid as a condition of sale, and are not already included in the price
options.B: Only if the importer is related to the seller
options.C: Only when the goods are sold after importation
options.D: Whenever any royalty exists anywhere in the company

questions.explanation: These are the three core royalty tests.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why may branch office transfers fail to qualify as sales?

options.A: Because customs forbids related-party trade
options.B: Because branches never import goods
options.C: Because the branch office and parent company may not be separate legal entities, so there is no sale between separate persons
options.D: Because they always use fallback method

questions.explanation: A transfer within the same legal person is not a sale.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are payments for the right to distribute or resell imported goods always added to customs value?

options.A: Not automatically; they are not added unless they are a condition of sale of the imported goods
options.B: Only if transport was CIF
options.C: No, never
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: Distribution/resale rights require further analysis.

questions.question: What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Only Article 8 adjustments
options.B: The six valuation methods
options.C: Only related-party rules
options.D: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Which document is especially important for establishing transaction value?

options.A: The warehouse ledger
options.B: The commercial invoice
options.C: The employee contract
options.D: The inland delivery note only

questions.explanation: The commercial invoice is central to transaction value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can duties and taxes of the importing country be excluded from customs value if distinguished from the price?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if transport is by air
options.C: Only if the seller is unrelated
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Import-country duties and taxes may be excluded if separated.

questions.question: Can discounted sales still qualify for transaction value?

options.A: Only trade discounts are allowed
options.B: Only cash discounts are allowed
options.C: Yes, cash, volume, trade, and purchase discounts do not automatically disqualify transaction value
options.D: No, discounts always invalidate Method 1

questions.explanation: Discounts may still be part of a valid actual price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is Method 1 of customs valuation?

options.A: Transaction value
options.B: Fallback method
options.C: Transaction value of identical goods
options.D: Computed value

questions.explanation: Method 1 is transaction value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is another way to show no price influence in a related-party sale?

options.A: Show that the goods moved by road
options.B: Show that the price is the same as in sales to unrelated buyers in comparable circumstances
options.C: Show that the buyer paid late
options.D: Show that the importer used a letter of credit

questions.explanation: Comparable sales to unrelated buyers can support the price.

questions.question: An importer pays a trademark royalty tied to the imported goods, and the seller requires that payment as a condition of sale. The royalty is not already in the invoice. What is the likely result?

options.A: The royalty may need to be added to customs value
options.B: It becomes a post-import deduction
options.C: It must always be excluded
options.D: It is irrelevant because trademarks never matter

questions.explanation: Royalties may be dutiable if they meet the Article 8 tests.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What major shift occurred in the Tokyo Round regarding customs valuation?

options.A: It required minimum values for all imports
options.B: It replaced customs value with domestic market price
options.C: It adopted a positive valuation system based on the price actually paid or payable for imported goods
options.D: It abolished all valuation methods

questions.explanation: The Tokyo Round moved valuation toward the actual transaction price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can payment be made indirectly and still form part of the price actually paid or payable?

options.A: Only if paid after importation
options.B: Yes
options.C: No
options.D: Only if related parties are involved

questions.explanation: Indirect payments can be part of the price.

questions.question: Give one example of a problematic condition in Article 1.1(b).

options.A: The buyer requests standard commercial packing
options.B: The buyer pays through a bank
options.C: The seller issues a commercial invoice
options.D: The seller sets the price on condition that the buyer also buys other goods in specified quantities

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions can create valuation problems.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? How many main methods of customs valuation are provided under the Agreement?

options.A: Five methods
options.B: Six methods
options.C: Seven methods
options.D: Four methods

questions.explanation: There are six valuation methods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What was the Brussels Definition of Value (BDV)?

options.A: A type of transport insurance
options.B: A rule on customs warehousing
options.C: The current WTO fallback method
options.D: An earlier customs valuation concept developed before the WTO transaction value system became dominant

questions.explanation: BDV was an older valuation concept.

questions.question: Customs wants to verify whether the invoice price already includes insurance and freight up to the place of importation. Which type of information is most directly helpful?

options.A: The importer’s family relationship only
options.B: The importer’s domestic advertising budget
options.C: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents
options.D: The number of customs officers involved

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why is greater uniformity and certainty important in customs valuation?

options.A: Because traders should know the rules in advance and trust that valuation will be applied consistently and fairly
options.B: Because customs officers should have unlimited discretion
options.C: Because valuation should vary by port
options.D: Because every importer must pay the same amount

questions.explanation: Uniformity and certainty support predictable trade.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a document in the customs valuation lesson on documentation?

options.A: Only a paper invoice signed by customs
options.B: Only a printed customs declaration
options.C: Only a transport document
options.D: Presentation of data in digital or any other form for the purpose of exchanging information and communication

questions.explanation: The lesson uses a broad meaning of document.

questions.question: Give one example of a problematic condition in Article 1.1(b).

options.A: The buyer pays through a bank
options.B: The seller issues a commercial invoice
options.C: The seller sets the price on condition that the buyer also buys other goods in specified quantities
options.D: The buyer requests standard commercial packing

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions can create valuation problems.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? If goods are sold FOB, what must customs often verify?

options.A: Whether freight, insurance, and related charges to the place of importation are not included and therefore need treatment under Article 8.2
options.B: Whether a buying commission becomes dutiable
options.C: Whether the importer owns the vessel
options.D: Whether customs value must always be rejected

questions.explanation: FOB often excludes main carriage and insurance.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one typical function of a buying agent.

options.A: Issuing bills of lading
options.B: Finding suppliers of the goods wanted by the buyer
options.C: Assessing customs duty
options.D: Setting tariff rates

questions.explanation: A buying agent helps the importer source goods.

questions.question: What is another type of allowable restriction under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Restrictions limiting the geographical area in which the goods may be resold
options.B: Restrictions forcing the buyer to purchase unrelated goods
options.C: Restrictions requiring arbitrary reference prices
options.D: Restrictions whose value cannot be determined

questions.explanation: Geographical resale restrictions may be allowed.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is brokerage in customs valuation?

options.A: A duty charged by customs
options.B: A fee paid to a broker who brings buyer and seller together and arranges the deal
options.C: A warehouse storage fee
options.D: A post-import transport cost

questions.explanation: Brokerage relates to arranging the transaction.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one type of legal deduction that may be excluded if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable.

options.A: Containers and packing
options.B: Assists supplied by the buyer
options.C: Commissions to the seller’s agent
options.D: Charges for construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance undertaken after importation

questions.explanation: Post-importation service charges may be excluded if properly distinguished.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Goods are shipped by a foreign supplier to a Kenyan distributor on consignment, with ownership remaining abroad until local resale. Can Article 1 transaction value normally be used at importation?

options.A: Yes, if a packing list is available
options.B: Only if the goods are insured
options.C: Usually no, because there may be no sale for export at the time of importation
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: Consignment often means no completed sale at the moment of importation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What are assists in customs valuation?

options.A: The importer’s unrelated marketing expenses
options.B: Any service provided by customs
options.C: Only post-import repair services
options.D: Goods or services supplied directly or indirectly by the buyer free of charge or at reduced cost for use in producing and selling the imported goods for export

questions.explanation: Assists are buyer-provided inputs or services tied to production or sale for export.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer undertakes advertising in the importing country on its own account, and it is not a condition of sale. Does this automatically bar transaction value?

options.A: Only if the buyer is unrelated
options.B: No
options.C: Yes, because it is always an assist
options.D: Yes, every marketing activity bars Method 1

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities do not automatically prevent Method 1.

questions.question: Do conditions relating to production or marketing of the imported goods always cause rejection of transaction value?

options.A: No, some such conditions do not prevent use of transaction value
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Only if transport is by sea
options.D: Yes, unless the parties are unrelated

questions.explanation: Not every condition is disqualifying.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Which GATT article is the legal foundation for customs valuation?

options.A: Article V
options.B: Article X
options.C: Article VII of GATT 1994
options.D: Article VIII

questions.explanation: Article VII is the legal base for customs valuation.

questions.question: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB means insurance is always included
options.B: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated
options.C: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.D: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: Do payments for the right to reproduce imported goods in the country of importation always form part of customs value?

options.A: Only if the importer is unrelated
options.B: Yes
options.C: No
options.D: Only if paid in local currency

questions.explanation: Reproduction rights in the importing country are generally excluded.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is the third category of allowable restriction under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Restrictions based on domestic market prices
options.B: Restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods
options.C: Restrictions imposed by a parent company automatically
options.D: Restrictions that increase customs revenue

questions.explanation: Insignificant restrictions do not bar Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the third category of allowable restriction under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods
options.B: Restrictions based on domestic market prices
options.C: Restrictions imposed by a parent company automatically
options.D: Restrictions that increase customs revenue

questions.explanation: Insignificant restrictions do not bar Method 1.

questions.question: What is a document check?

options.A: A customs court hearing
options.B: A systematic analysis of all supporting documents to a customs declaration to facilitate proper valuation of imported goods
options.C: A physical inspection of the goods only
options.D: A post-import warehouse audit only

questions.explanation: Document check is a systematic review of supporting papers and data.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why are documents important in customs valuation?

options.A: They replace the need for Article 1 analysis
options.B: They matter only for packaging decisions
options.C: They are relevant only after customs has fixed the value
options.D: They support clearance, origin identification, statistics, audit, intelligence, dispute settlement, revenue collection, and proof of ownership

questions.explanation: Documents support many customs functions including valuation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one category of related persons under Article 15.

options.A: Members of the same family
options.B: Any seller and carrier
options.C: Any two importers in the same port
options.D: Any broker and customs officer

questions.explanation: Family members are one related-party category.

questions.question: If goods are sold FOB, what must customs often verify?

options.A: Whether a buying commission becomes dutiable
options.B: Whether the importer owns the vessel
options.C: Whether freight, insurance, and related charges to the place of importation are not included and therefore need treatment under Article 8.2
options.D: Whether customs value must always be rejected

questions.explanation: FOB often excludes main carriage and insurance.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? If goods are sold FOB, what must customs often verify?

options.A: Whether customs value must always be rejected
options.B: Whether freight, insurance, and related charges to the place of importation are not included and therefore need treatment under Article 8.2
options.C: Whether the importer owns the vessel
options.D: Whether a buying commission becomes dutiable

questions.explanation: FOB often excludes main carriage and insurance.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Part III of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Importer’s right to written explanation
options.B: Publication requirements
options.C: Special and differential treatment in Article 20
options.D: Currency conversion

questions.explanation: Part III is the S&D section.

questions.question: What stockholding threshold is used in one related-party test?

options.A: One percent
options.B: Fifty percent
options.C: Ten percent
options.D: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person

questions.explanation: The notes mention a five-percent threshold.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What four questions help test whether transaction value is acceptable under Article 1.1?

options.A: Whether there are restrictions on disposition or use, undeterminable conditions, proceeds accruing to the seller, and whether buyer and seller are related
options.B: Whether the importer has an appeal right, a broker, a bank, and an agent
options.C: Whether the goods are insured, packaged, labeled, and warehoused
options.D: Whether the goods are perishable, bulky, identical, or exempt

questions.explanation: These are the four major Article 1.1 tests.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What happens if there is no quantifiable data for a proposed Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The goods must be reclassified
options.B: Customs must estimate it upward
options.C: The importer must accept a minimum value
options.D: The adjustment cannot be made under transaction value

questions.explanation: Without quantifiable data, the addition cannot be made.

questions.question: How are warranty costs treated when already included in the selling price of imported goods?

options.A: They are always deducted
options.B: They are treated as post-import costs automatically
options.C: They form part of the customs value because they are part of the price actually paid or payable
options.D: They are ignored completely

questions.explanation: If already included in the price, they remain part of it.

tests.title: Customs Valuation I - Main Test 2
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Customs Valuation I notes.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: What did the Uruguay Round achieve in customs valuation?

options.A: It removed Article 8 adjustments
options.B: It produced the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement and strengthened the transaction value system as the main method
options.C: It made minimum values compulsory
options.D: It reintroduced BDV as the preferred method

questions.explanation: The Uruguay Round created the WTO system.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What are the key conditions for an Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The cost must always be estimated by customs
options.B: The goods must be identical goods
options.C: The criteria must be met, the cost incurred by the buyer, not already included in the price, and based on objective and quantifiable data
options.D: The seller must request the addition

questions.explanation: These are the general rules for adjustments.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What kind of criteria should customs value be based on according to the Agreement?

options.A: Simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practices
options.B: Domestic production costs only
options.C: The highest of two alternative values
options.D: Arbitrary benchmark prices

questions.explanation: The Agreement emphasizes simple and equitable criteria.

questions.question: What must exist before Method 1 can be used?

options.A: A customs warehouse entry
options.B: A transport contract only
options.C: A resale in the importing country
options.D: A sale for export of the goods to the country of importation

questions.explanation: Method 1 requires a sale for export.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Are selling commissions added to customs value if incurred by the buyer and not already included?

options.A: Only if transport is by sea
options.B: Only if the importer refuses to disclose them
options.C: Yes
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Selling commissions may be added.

questions.question: A company imports goods from its foreign parent company. Customs discovers they are related parties. What is the most important question before rejecting Method 1?

options.A: Whether the goods moved by sea
options.B: Whether the invoice was discounted
options.C: Whether the importer used a buying agent
options.D: Whether the relationship influenced the price

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key issue is influence on price.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: To require all Members to use fixed benchmark prices
options.B: To create a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing imported goods and prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values
options.C: To replace all importer appeal rights
options.D: To maximize customs revenue at any cost

questions.explanation: This is the central objective of the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is Article 8 mainly about?

options.A: Adjustments to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value
options.B: The appeal procedure only
options.C: The sequence of methods 2 to 6 only
options.D: The legal definition of sale only

questions.explanation: Article 8 governs additions to the price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What stockholding threshold is used in one related-party test?

options.A: Fifty percent
options.B: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person
options.C: One percent
options.D: Ten percent

questions.explanation: The notes mention a five-percent threshold.

questions.question: Name the fourth category of assists.

options.A: Domestic resale contracts
options.B: Customs declarations filed by the importer
options.C: Engineering, development, artwork, design work, and plans and sketches undertaken outside the country of importation and necessary for production
options.D: Import-country tax advice

questions.explanation: This category covers certain external design and development work.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Are payments for the right to distribute or resell imported goods always added to customs value?

options.A: Not automatically; they are not added unless they are a condition of sale of the imported goods
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: No, never
options.D: Only if transport was CIF

questions.explanation: Distribution/resale rights require further analysis.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name another category of assists.

options.A: Buying commissions
options.B: Administrative penalties
options.C: Post-import warehousing
options.D: Tools, dies, moulds, and similar items used in production of the imported goods

questions.explanation: Production tools can be assists.

questions.question: What kind of criteria should customs value be based on according to the Agreement?

options.A: Simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practices
options.B: Domestic production costs only
options.C: Arbitrary benchmark prices
options.D: The highest of two alternative values

questions.explanation: The Agreement emphasizes simple and equitable criteria.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Only Article 8 adjustments
options.B: The six valuation methods
options.C: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24
options.D: Only related-party rules

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are dividends paid by the buyer to the seller always part of the customs value?

options.A: Yes, always
options.B: Yes, if they are paid after importation
options.C: Yes, whenever the buyer and seller are related
options.D: No, not if they do not relate to the imported goods

questions.explanation: Dividends unrelated to the goods are not part of customs value.

questions.question: Goods are shipped by a foreign supplier to a Kenyan distributor on consignment, with ownership remaining abroad until local resale. Can Article 1 transaction value normally be used at importation?

options.A: Only if the goods are insured
options.B: Usually no, because there may be no sale for export at the time of importation
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: Yes, if a packing list is available

questions.explanation: Consignment often means no completed sale at the moment of importation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Can discounted sales still qualify for transaction value?

options.A: Only trade discounts are allowed
options.B: Only cash discounts are allowed
options.C: No, discounts always invalidate Method 1
options.D: Yes, cash, volume, trade, and purchase discounts do not automatically disqualify transaction value

questions.explanation: Discounts may still be part of a valid actual price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 2 of customs valuation?

options.A: Transaction value of identical goods
options.B: Computed value
options.C: Transaction value of similar goods
options.D: Deductive value

questions.explanation: Method 2 is identical goods.

questions.question: What happens if there is no quantifiable data for a proposed Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The importer must accept a minimum value
options.B: The adjustment cannot be made under transaction value
options.C: Customs must estimate it upward
options.D: The goods must be reclassified

questions.explanation: Without quantifiable data, the addition cannot be made.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What stockholding threshold is used in one related-party test?

options.A: Ten percent
options.B: Fifty percent
options.C: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person
options.D: One percent

questions.explanation: The notes mention a five-percent threshold.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How are warranty costs treated when already included in the selling price of imported goods?

options.A: They form part of the customs value because they are part of the price actually paid or payable
options.B: They are treated as post-import costs automatically
options.C: They are always deducted
options.D: They are ignored completely

questions.explanation: If already included in the price, they remain part of it.

questions.question: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: Customs must apply minimum values
options.B: Customs must reject Method 1 because the price is low
options.C: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it
options.D: Customs must ignore the supporting documents

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer undertakes advertising in the importing country on its own account, and it is not a condition of sale. Does this automatically bar transaction value?

options.A: Yes, because it is always an assist
options.B: Only if the buyer is unrelated
options.C: Yes, every marketing activity bars Method 1
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities do not automatically prevent Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is customs value important?

options.A: Because customs value only matters for warehousing
options.B: Because duties and taxes, especially ad valorem duties, are calculated using customs value as the assessment base
options.C: Because customs value replaces classification
options.D: Because customs value determines nationality of the importer

questions.explanation: Ad valorem duties depend on customs value.

questions.question: What is the third category of allowable restriction under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Restrictions based on domestic market prices
options.B: Restrictions imposed by a parent company automatically
options.C: Restrictions that increase customs revenue
options.D: Restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods

questions.explanation: Insignificant restrictions do not bar Method 1.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Which methods may sometimes be reversed at the importer’s request?

options.A: Identical goods and similar goods
options.B: Deductive value and computed value, meaning Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed in order
options.C: Computed value and fallback
options.D: Transaction value and identical goods

questions.explanation: Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed on request.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why are documents important in customs valuation?

options.A: They support clearance, origin identification, statistics, audit, intelligence, dispute settlement, revenue collection, and proof of ownership
options.B: They are relevant only after customs has fixed the value
options.C: They replace the need for Article 1 analysis
options.D: They matter only for packaging decisions

questions.explanation: Documents support many customs functions including valuation.

questions.question: Why is a packing list useful in customs valuation?

options.A: Because it replaces a commercial invoice
options.B: Because it proves the seller’s profit margin
options.C: Because it supports quantity, packaging, marks, numbers, and content checks against the invoice and transport documents
options.D: Because it sets exchange rates

questions.explanation: The packing list helps cross-check physical shipment details.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What are assists in customs valuation?

options.A: Any service provided by customs
options.B: Only post-import repair services
options.C: The importer’s unrelated marketing expenses
options.D: Goods or services supplied directly or indirectly by the buyer free of charge or at reduced cost for use in producing and selling the imported goods for export

questions.explanation: Assists are buyer-provided inputs or services tied to production or sale for export.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the main objective of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: To require all Members to use fixed benchmark prices
options.B: To maximize customs revenue at any cost
options.C: To create a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing imported goods and prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 6 of customs valuation?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why is a packing list useful in customs valuation?

options.A: Because it replaces a commercial invoice
options.B: Because it sets exchange rates

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What major shift occurred in the Tokyo Round regarding customs valuation?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Can discounted sales still qualify for transaction value?

options.D: Only cash discounts are allowed

options.A: Because the branch office and parent company may not be separate legal entities, so there is no sale between separate persons
options.B: Because customs forbids related-party trade
options.C: Because branches never import goods
options.D: Because they always use fallback method

questions.explanation: A transfer within the same legal person is not a sale.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can transaction value still be used where proceeds accrue to the seller?

options.A: Only if the goods are not resold
options.B: Yes, if an appropriate adjustment can be made under Article 8
options.C: No, never
options.D: Only if the importer waives appeal rights

questions.explanation: Relevant proceeds may be handled through Article 8 adjustment.

questions.question: How many main methods of customs valuation are provided under the Agreement?

options.A: Five methods
options.B: Four methods
options.C: Six methods
options.D: Seven methods

questions.explanation: There are six valuation methods.

questions.question: The buyer undertakes advertising in the importing country on its own account, and it is not a condition of sale. Does this automatically bar transaction value?

options.A: Yes, because it is always an assist
options.B: Only if the buyer is unrelated
options.C: Yes, every marketing activity bars Method 1
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities do not automatically prevent Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 3 of customs valuation?

options.A: Transaction value of similar goods
options.B: Deductive value
options.C: Computed value
options.D: Fallback method

questions.explanation: Method 3 is similar goods.

questions.question: What happens if there is no quantifiable data for a proposed Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The goods must be reclassified
options.B: The adjustment cannot be made under transaction value
options.C: The importer must accept a minimum value
options.D: Customs must estimate it upward

questions.explanation: Without quantifiable data, the addition cannot be made.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? If goods are supplied on loan and remain the exporter’s property, is there a sale?

options.A: Only if the goods are identical goods
options.B: Yes
options.C: No
options.D: Only if the buyer insures the goods

questions.explanation: A loan without transfer of ownership is not a sale.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs add amounts not listed in Article 8?

options.A: Yes, whenever revenue is low
options.B: Yes, if they seem commercially important
options.C: Yes, if customs issues a circular
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Article 8 is a closed list for additions.

questions.question: What does Part II of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Only special and differential treatment
options.B: Administration, consultations, and dispute settlement in Articles 18 and 19
options.C: Valuation methods only
options.D: Only definitions and annexes

questions.explanation: Part II addresses administration and disputes.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Are payments for the right to distribute or resell imported goods always added to customs value?

options.A: Only if transport was CIF
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Not automatically; they are not added unless they are a condition of sale of the imported goods
options.D: No, never

questions.explanation: Distribution/resale rights require further analysis.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name the fourth category of assists.

options.A: Customs declarations filed by the importer
options.B: Domestic resale contracts
options.C: Import-country tax advice
options.D: Engineering, development, artwork, design work, and plans and sketches undertaken outside the country of importation and necessary for production

questions.explanation: This category covers certain external design and development work.

questions.question: Name one type of legal deduction that may be excluded if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable.

options.A: Charges for construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance undertaken after importation
options.B: Assists supplied by the buyer
options.C: Commissions to the seller’s agent
options.D: Containers and packing

questions.explanation: Post-importation service charges may be excluded if properly distinguished.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why are terms of payment important in valuation documents?

options.A: Because they always change the country of origin
options.B: Because they determine the HS classification
options.C: Because they help explain how and when the buyer pays the seller, which can affect analysis of the price actually paid or payable
options.D: Because they decide whether goods are identical

questions.explanation: Payment terms help interpret the actual price arrangement.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the three key elements for applying transaction value according to the notes?

options.A: The tariff rate, the origin mark, and the HS code
options.B: The transport mode, insurance, and customs office
options.C: The duty amount, warehousing period, and bond
options.D: The sale, the sale for export to the country of importation, and the price actually paid or payable

questions.explanation: These are the three core elements.

questions.question: What is Method 1 of customs valuation?

options.A: Transaction value of identical goods
options.B: Transaction value
options.C: Fallback method
options.D: Computed value

questions.explanation: Method 1 is transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What are the two broad ways of examining related-party transaction value?

options.A: By observing the circumstances surrounding the sale or by measuring against test values
options.B: By applying Article 8 directly without inquiry
options.C: By comparing only with transport costs
options.D: By using minimum values or domestic prices

questions.explanation: These are the two accepted approaches.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a buying commission?

options.A: A fee paid to a transport carrier
options.B: A customs brokerage fee at the port
options.C: A fee paid by the importer to the importer’s agent for representing the importer abroad in purchasing the goods
options.D: A fee paid to the seller’s agent to secure the sale

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are fees to the buyer’s own purchasing agent.

questions.question: How should the value of an assist be apportioned?

options.A: In a reasonable manner appropriate to the circumstances and supported by documentation
options.B: By customs estimate alone
options.C: Only over the first shipment
options.D: Always equally over all future imports regardless of evidence

questions.explanation: Assist allocation must be reasonable and documented.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Can consignment sales qualify as a sale for transaction value at importation?

options.A: Only if transport is by sea
options.B: Usually no, because ownership may remain with the foreign supplier at the time of importation
options.C: Yes, if the buyer requests a discount
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: Consignment usually does not involve a completed sale at importation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one type of legal deduction that may be excluded if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable.

options.A: Containers and packing
options.B: Assists supplied by the buyer
options.C: Commissions to the seller’s agent
options.D: Charges for construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance undertaken after importation

questions.explanation: Post-importation service charges may be excluded if properly distinguished.

questions.question: Can payment be made indirectly and still form part of the price actually paid or payable?

options.A: Yes
options.B: Only if paid after importation
options.C: Only if related parties are involved
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Indirect payments can be part of the price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is an ad valorem duty?

options.A: A duty charged per package regardless of value
options.B: A duty charged as a percentage of the customs value of imported goods
options.C: A transport charge by the carrier
options.D: A fee for post-clearance audit

questions.explanation: Ad valorem means based on value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the main objective of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: To replace all importer appeal rights
options.B: To maximize customs revenue at any cost
options.C: To require all Members to use fixed benchmark prices
options.D: To create a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing imported goods and prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values

questions.explanation: This is the central objective of the Agreement.

questions.question: What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: The six valuation methods
options.B: Only Article 8 adjustments
options.C: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24
options.D: Only related-party rules

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What was the Brussels Definition of Value (BDV)?

options.A: A rule on customs warehousing
options.B: An earlier customs valuation concept developed before the WTO transaction value system became dominant
options.C: A type of transport insurance
options.D: The current WTO fallback method

questions.explanation: BDV was an older valuation concept.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can payment be made indirectly and still form part of the price actually paid or payable?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if related parties are involved
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only if paid after importation

questions.explanation: Indirect payments can be part of the price.

questions.question: Name one category of assists under Article 8.1(b).

options.A: Post-import transport
options.B: Import-country duties and taxes
options.C: Buyer’s domestic advertising
options.D: Materials, components, parts, and similar items incorporated in the imported goods

questions.explanation: This is one assist category.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Are selling commissions added to customs value if incurred by the buyer and not already included?

options.A: Yes
options.B: Only if transport is by sea
options.C: No
options.D: Only if the importer refuses to disclose them

questions.explanation: Selling commissions may be added.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one way to show that a related-party price was not influenced by the relationship?

options.A: Show that duties were already paid
options.B: Show that the invoice is handwritten
options.C: Show that transport costs are high
options.D: Show that the price is consistent with normal industry pricing practice

questions.explanation: Industry practice can support acceptability.

questions.question: A company imports goods from its foreign parent company. Customs discovers they are related parties. What is the most important question before rejecting Method 1?

options.A: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.B: Whether the importer used a buying agent
options.C: Whether the invoice was discounted
options.D: Whether the goods moved by sea

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key issue is influence on price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is meant by proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use under Article 8.1(d)?

options.A: Any customs penalty imposed after clearance
options.B: A payment or value from later resale, disposal, or use of the imported goods that accrues directly or indirectly to the seller
options.C: Any domestic resale profit kept entirely by the importer
options.D: Any freight refund from the carrier

questions.explanation: Proceeds must accrue to the seller.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a buying commission?

options.A: A customs brokerage fee at the port
options.B: A fee paid to the seller’s agent to secure the sale
options.C: A fee paid by the importer to the importer’s agent for representing the importer abroad in purchasing the goods
options.D: A fee paid to a transport carrier

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are fees to the buyer’s own purchasing agent.

questions.question: Give one example of a problematic condition in Article 1.1(b).

options.A: The buyer requests standard commercial packing
options.B: The seller sets the price on condition that the buyer also buys other goods in specified quantities
options.C: The buyer pays through a bank
options.D: The seller issues a commercial invoice

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions can create valuation problems.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What does it mean if customs concludes there was no price influence in a related-party sale?

options.A: The importer loses the right to appeal
options.B: Customs must automatically use fallback
options.C: The price may be treated as arm’s length and transaction value may be accepted
options.D: Customs must still reject the value

questions.explanation: A related-party sale may still qualify if price was not influenced.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Part II of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Administration, consultations, and dispute settlement in Articles 18 and 19
options.B: Only definitions and annexes
options.C: Valuation methods only
options.D: Only special and differential treatment

questions.explanation: Part II addresses administration and disputes.

questions.question: Can discounted sales still qualify for transaction value?

options.A: Only trade discounts are allowed
options.B: No, discounts always invalidate Method 1
options.C: Only cash discounts are allowed
options.D: Yes, cash, volume, trade, and purchase discounts do not automatically disqualify transaction value

questions.explanation: Discounts may still be part of a valid actual price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why are Incoterms or terms of delivery important in valuation documents?

options.A: Because they replace Article 8
options.B: Because they help show who bears freight, insurance, handling, and related costs and whether those amounts are already in the invoice price
options.C: Because they determine the importer’s nationality
options.D: Because they set customs appeal rights

questions.explanation: Incoterms help interpret the price structure.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can discounted sales still qualify for transaction value?

options.A: Only trade discounts are allowed
options.B: No, discounts always invalidate Method 1
options.C: Only cash discounts are allowed
options.D: Yes, cash, volume, trade, and purchase discounts do not automatically disqualify transaction value

questions.explanation: Discounts may still be part of a valid actual price.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A post-importation cost deduction
options.B: A permitted geographical resale restriction
options.C: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.D: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why are documents important in customs valuation?

options.A: They support clearance, origin identification, statistics, audit, intelligence, dispute settlement, revenue collection, and proof of ownership
options.B: They matter only for packaging decisions
options.C: They are relevant only after customs has fixed the value
options.D: They replace the need for Article 1 analysis

questions.explanation: Documents support many customs functions including valuation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are the cost of containers and the cost of packing possible Article 8 additions?

options.A: Only if the goods are insured
options.B: Yes
options.C: No
options.D: Only for air shipments

questions.explanation: Containers and packing may be additions.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: To create a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing imported goods and prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values
options.B: To replace all importer appeal rights
options.C: To maximize customs revenue at any cost
options.D: To require all Members to use fixed benchmark prices

questions.explanation: This is the central objective of the Agreement.

tests.title: Customs Valuation I - Main Test 5
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Customs Valuation I notes.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Why must customs examine agency arrangements carefully?

options.A: Because buying commissions are always dutiable
options.B: Because customs must replace all agents
options.C: Because agency is never allowed in trade
options.D: Because fees labeled as buying commissions may actually include services benefiting the seller or extend beyond true buying-agent functions

questions.explanation: Substance matters more than the label.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is Method 4 of customs valuation?

options.A: Deductive value
options.B: Computed value
options.C: Fallback method
options.D: Transaction value

questions.explanation: Method 4 is deductive value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are payments for the right to distribute or resell imported goods always added to customs value?

options.A: No, never
options.B: Only if transport was CIF
options.C: Not automatically; they are not added unless they are a condition of sale of the imported goods
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: Distribution/resale rights require further analysis.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated
options.B: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
options.C: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.D: Because FOB means insurance is always included

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What major shift occurred in the Tokyo Round regarding customs valuation?

options.A: It replaced customs value with domestic market price
options.B: It required minimum values for all imports
options.C: It adopted a positive valuation system based on the price actually paid or payable for imported goods
options.D: It abolished all valuation methods

questions.explanation: The Tokyo Round moved valuation toward the actual transaction price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the price actually paid or payable?

options.A: Only payments made after customs clearance
options.B: Only cash paid physically to the seller
options.C: Only the invoice subtotal excluding all conditions of sale
options.D: The total payment made or to be made by the buyer to, or for the benefit of, the seller for the imported goods

questions.explanation: This includes direct and indirect payments for the goods.

questions.question: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: Customs must reject Method 1 because the price is low
options.B: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it
options.C: Customs must ignore the supporting documents
options.D: Customs must apply minimum values

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays a trademark royalty tied to the imported goods, and the seller requires that payment as a condition of sale. The royalty is not already in the invoice. What is the likely result?

options.A: The royalty may need to be added to customs value
options.B: It becomes a post-import deduction
options.C: It must always be excluded
options.D: It is irrelevant because trademarks never matter

questions.explanation: Royalties may be dutiable if they meet the Article 8 tests.

questions.question: A proposed addition is mentioned by the customs officer, but there is no objective and quantifiable data to support the amount. Can the addition be made under Method 1?

options.A: Yes, customs may estimate it freely
options.B: Yes, if the shipment is split
options.C: Yes, if the importer is related
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Without objective and quantifiable data, the adjustment cannot be made.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A proposed addition is mentioned by the customs officer, but there is no objective and quantifiable data to support the amount. Can the addition be made under Method 1?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes, customs may estimate it freely
options.C: Yes, if the shipment is split
options.D: Yes, if the importer is related

questions.explanation: Without objective and quantifiable data, the adjustment cannot be made.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is the price actually paid or payable?

options.A: Only payments made after customs clearance
options.B: The total payment made or to be made by the buyer to, or for the benefit of, the seller for the imported goods
options.C: Only cash paid physically to the seller
options.D: Only the invoice subtotal excluding all conditions of sale

questions.explanation: This includes direct and indirect payments for the goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Part III of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Publication requirements
options.B: Importer’s right to written explanation
options.C: Special and differential treatment in Article 20
options.D: Currency conversion

questions.explanation: Part III is the S&D section.

questions.question: Can discounted sales still qualify for transaction value?

options.A: Only cash discounts are allowed
options.B: Yes, cash, volume, trade, and purchase discounts do not automatically disqualify transaction value
options.C: No, discounts always invalidate Method 1
options.D: Only trade discounts are allowed

questions.explanation: Discounts may still be part of a valid actual price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is the second key test for adding a royalty or licence fee?

options.A: The buyer must have to pay it directly or indirectly as a condition of sale of the imported goods
options.B: It must be voluntary and unrelated to the sale
options.C: The buyer must always pay it after resale
options.D: It must be a domestic tax

questions.explanation: Condition of sale is the second test.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are selling commissions added to customs value if incurred by the buyer and not already included?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if transport is by sea
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only if the importer refuses to disclose them

questions.explanation: Selling commissions may be added.

questions.question: What did the Uruguay Round achieve in customs valuation?

options.A: It reintroduced BDV as the preferred method
options.B: It made minimum values compulsory
options.C: It removed Article 8 adjustments
options.D: It produced the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement and strengthened the transaction value system as the main method

questions.explanation: The Uruguay Round created the WTO system.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What does Part II of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Valuation methods only
options.B: Only special and differential treatment
options.C: Administration, consultations, and dispute settlement in Articles 18 and 19
options.D: Only definitions and annexes

questions.explanation: Part II addresses administration and disputes.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays its overseas sourcing representative to find suppliers and negotiate prices. What is the likely treatment if this is a true buying commission?

options.A: It is generally excluded from customs value
options.B: It must always be added
options.C: It is automatically a proceed of resale
options.D: It is treated as a royalty

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are excluded when genuinely paid to the buyer’s own agent.

questions.question: Which document is especially important for establishing transaction value?

options.A: The inland delivery note only
options.B: The employee contract
options.C: The warehouse ledger
options.D: The commercial invoice

questions.explanation: The commercial invoice is central to transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What are assists in customs valuation?

options.A: The importer’s unrelated marketing expenses
options.B: Goods or services supplied directly or indirectly by the buyer free of charge or at reduced cost for use in producing and selling the imported goods for export
options.C: Any service provided by customs
options.D: Only post-import repair services

questions.explanation: Assists are buyer-provided inputs or services tied to production or sale for export.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the three key elements for applying transaction value according to the notes?

options.A: The sale, the sale for export to the country of importation, and the price actually paid or payable
options.B: The duty amount, warehousing period, and bond
options.C: The transport mode, insurance, and customs office
options.D: The tariff rate, the origin mark, and the HS code

questions.explanation: These are the three core elements.

questions.question: Why are documents important in customs valuation?

options.A: They replace the need for Article 1 analysis
options.B: They are relevant only after customs has fixed the value
options.C: They matter only for packaging decisions
options.D: They support clearance, origin identification, statistics, audit, intelligence, dispute settlement, revenue collection, and proof of ownership

questions.explanation: Documents support many customs functions including valuation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why is a bill of lading important in customs valuation?

options.A: Because it is always a proof of payment
options.B: Because it can help establish shipment details, freight terms, shipment timing, vessel information, and international movement of the goods
options.C: Because it shows the domestic retail price
options.D: Because it determines the import duty rate

questions.explanation: The bill of lading supports shipment analysis.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Part III of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Currency conversion
options.B: Importer’s right to written explanation
options.C: Special and differential treatment in Article 20
options.D: Publication requirements

questions.explanation: Part III is the S&D section.

questions.question: How many main methods of customs valuation are provided under the Agreement?

options.A: Seven methods
options.B: Five methods
options.C: Four methods
options.D: Six methods

questions.explanation: There are six valuation methods.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why is the commercial invoice important in customs valuation?

options.A: Because it proves insurance cover only
options.B: Because it usually contains the seller, buyer, price, quantity, terms of sale, and other details central to the price actually paid or payable
options.C: Because it replaces the sales contract in all cases
options.D: Because it states the customs officer’s final value

questions.explanation: The invoice provides essential valuation details.

questions.question: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: Customs must ignore the supporting documents
options.B: Customs must reject Method 1 because the price is low
options.C: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it
options.D: Customs must apply minimum values

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: What is one way to show that a related-party price was not influenced by the relationship?

options.A: Show that the price is consistent with normal industry pricing practice
options.B: Show that duties were already paid
options.C: Show that transport costs are high
options.D: Show that the invoice is handwritten

questions.explanation: Industry practice can support acceptability.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? When can royalties and licence fees be added to customs value?

options.A: Only if the importer is related to the seller
options.B: Whenever any royalty exists anywhere in the company
options.C: Only when the goods are sold after importation
options.D: When they relate to the goods being valued, are paid as a condition of sale, and are not already included in the price

questions.explanation: These are the three core royalty tests.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A post-importation cost deduction
options.B: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing
options.C: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.D: A permitted geographical resale restriction

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: Can duties and taxes of the importing country be excluded from customs value if distinguished from the price?

options.A: Yes
options.B: No
options.C: Only if transport is by air
options.D: Only if the seller is unrelated

questions.explanation: Import-country duties and taxes may be excluded if separated.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is a buying commission?

options.A: A fee paid to the seller’s agent to secure the sale
options.B: A fee paid by the importer to the importer’s agent for representing the importer abroad in purchasing the goods
options.C: A customs brokerage fee at the port
options.D: A fee paid to a transport carrier

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are fees to the buyer’s own purchasing agent.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Must the methods of customs valuation be applied in sequence?

options.A: No, customs may choose freely
options.B: Only the importer decides the order
options.C: Yes, the methods are applied sequentially
options.D: Only methods 1 to 3 must be followed in order

questions.explanation: The methods follow a sequence.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.B: A permitted geographical resale restriction
options.C: A post-importation cost deduction
options.D: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: Goods are shipped by a foreign supplier to a Kenyan distributor on consignment, with ownership remaining abroad until local resale. Can Article 1 transaction value normally be used at importation?

options.A: Yes, if a packing list is available
options.B: Only if the goods are insured
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: Usually no, because there may be no sale for export at the time of importation

questions.explanation: Consignment often means no completed sale at the moment of importation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Give the full recap formula for transaction value.

options.A: Transaction value equals the highest value customs can justify
options.B: Transaction value equals the price actually paid or payable for the goods when sold for export to the country of importation plus only those additions permitted under Article 8, subject to Article 1 conditions
options.C: Transaction value equals production cost plus profit in all cases
options.D: Transaction value equals domestic price plus all possible costs

questions.explanation: This recap captures the core Method 1 formula.

questions.question: How should the value of an assist be apportioned?

options.A: By customs estimate alone
options.B: Only over the first shipment
options.C: Always equally over all future imports regardless of evidence
options.D: In a reasonable manner appropriate to the circumstances and supported by documentation

questions.explanation: Assist allocation must be reasonable and documented.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What does 'sold for export' mean in customs valuation?

options.A: The goods were only offered for sale on a website
options.B: The goods were transported without a contract
options.C: The goods were sold in a transaction whose purpose was export to the country of importation
options.D: The goods were sold after importation in the domestic market

questions.explanation: The sale must be linked to export to the importing country.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can transaction value still be used where proceeds accrue to the seller?

options.D: Because they always use fallback method

questions.question: What does Annex I contain?

options.D: Only dispute settlement rules

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is the first key test for adding a royalty or licence fee?

options.A: It must be described as a dividend
options.B: It must be unrelated to the sale
options.C: It must be paid after importation
options.D: It must relate to the imported goods

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What stockholding threshold is used in one related-party test?

options.A: Fifty percent
options.B: One percent
options.C: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person
options.D: Ten percent

questions.explanation: The notes mention a five-percent threshold.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays a trademark royalty tied to the imported goods, and the seller requires that payment as a condition of sale. The royalty is not already in the invoice. What is the likely result?

options.A: The royalty may need to be added to customs value
options.B: It is irrelevant because trademarks never matter
options.C: It must always be excluded
options.D: It becomes a post-import deduction

questions.explanation: Royalties may be dutiable if they meet the Article 8 tests.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Are employer and employee related persons for customs valuation purposes?

options.A: Only if they are business partners
options.B: No
options.C: Only if they share voting stock
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Employer-employee is one related-party relationship.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it
options.B: Customs must ignore the supporting documents
options.C: Customs must reject Method 1 because the price is low
options.D: Customs must apply minimum values

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.B: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated
options.C: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
options.D: Because FOB means insurance is always included

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? If goods are supplied on loan and remain the exporter’s property, is there a sale?

options.A: Only if the buyer insures the goods
options.B: No
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only if the goods are identical goods

questions.explanation: A loan without transfer of ownership is not a sale.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What happens if there is no quantifiable data for a proposed Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The adjustment cannot be made under transaction value
options.B: Customs must estimate it upward

options.D: The goods must be reclassified

questions.explanation: Without quantifiable data, the addition cannot be made.

questions.question: Can customs add amounts not listed in Article 8?

options.A: Yes, if they seem commercially important
options.B: Yes, if customs issues a circular
options.C: Yes, whenever revenue is low
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Article 8 is a closed list for additions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What are the key conditions for an Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The goods must be identical goods
options.B: The criteria must be met, the cost incurred by the buyer, not already included in the price, and based on objective and quantifiable data
options.C: The cost must always be estimated by customs
options.D: The seller must request the addition

questions.explanation: These are the general rules for adjustments.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated
options.B: Because FOB means insurance is always included
options.C: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
