questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
options.B: Because FOB means insurance is always included
options.C: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.D: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A permitted geographical resale restriction
options.B: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.C: A post-importation cost deduction
options.D: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Do hire or leasing contracts normally constitute sales under the Agreement?

options.A: Yes, always
options.B: Only if they last more than one year
options.C: Only if customs requests it
options.D: No, leasing contracts are generally for rent or lease and not a sale

questions.explanation: Leasing is generally not a sale.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays a trademark royalty tied to the imported goods, and the seller requires that payment as a condition of sale. The royalty is not already in the invoice. What is the likely result?

options.A: It is irrelevant because trademarks never matter
options.B: It must always be excluded
options.C: The royalty may need to be added to customs value
options.D: It becomes a post-import deduction

questions.explanation: Royalties may be dutiable if they meet the Article 8 tests.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why can some conditions attached to the sale cause rejection of transaction value?

options.A: Because all conditions are prohibited
options.B: Because if the sale or price is subject to a condition whose value cannot be determined with respect to the imported goods, transaction value cannot be used
options.C: Because customs must always use fallback when conditions exist
options.D: Because conditions never matter under Article 1

questions.explanation: Undeterminable conditions can defeat Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 1 of customs valuation?

options.A: Computed value
options.B: Fallback method
options.C: Transaction value of identical goods
options.D: Transaction value

questions.explanation: Method 1 is transaction value.

questions.question: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A permitted geographical resale restriction
options.B: A post-importation cost deduction
options.C: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.D: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Are selling commissions added to customs value if incurred by the buyer and not already included?

options.A: Yes
options.B: No
options.C: Only if transport is by sea
options.D: Only if the importer refuses to disclose them

questions.explanation: Selling commissions may be added.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What major shift occurred in the Tokyo Round regarding customs valuation?

options.A: It replaced customs value with domestic market price
options.B: It adopted a positive valuation system based on the price actually paid or payable for imported goods
options.C: It abolished all valuation methods
options.D: It required minimum values for all imports

questions.explanation: The Tokyo Round moved valuation toward the actual transaction price.

questions.question: What does Part IV of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Final provisions in Articles 21 to 24
options.B: Only Article 8 adjustments
options.C: Only related-party rules
options.D: The six valuation methods

questions.explanation: Part IV contains the final provisions.

questions.question: Goods are shipped by a foreign supplier to a Kenyan distributor on consignment, with ownership remaining abroad until local resale. Can Article 1 transaction value normally be used at importation?

options.A: Only if the goods are insured

options.C: Yes, always

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What happens if there is no quantifiable data for a proposed Article 8 adjustment?

options.B: Customs must estimate it upward

options.D: Issuing bills of lading

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is the commercial invoice important in customs valuation?

questions.question: What does 'sold for export' mean in customs valuation?

options.A: The goods were only offered for sale on a website

options.A: Only if a buying commission is involved
options.B: No
options.C: Only if the price is discounted
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Relationship alone is not enough.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A company imports goods from its foreign parent company. Customs discovers they are related parties. What is the most important question before rejecting Method 1?

options.A: Whether the invoice was discounted
options.B: Whether the goods moved by sea
options.C: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.D: Whether the importer used a buying agent

questions.question: Are selling commissions added to customs value if incurred by the buyer and not already included?

options.A: No

options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Selling commissions may be added.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it
options.B: Customs must apply minimum values
options.C: Customs must ignore the supporting documents
options.D: Customs must reject Method 1 because the price is low

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why can some conditions attached to the sale cause rejection of transaction value?

options.A: Because all conditions are prohibited
options.B: Because if the sale or price is subject to a condition whose value cannot be determined with respect to the imported goods, transaction value cannot be used
options.C: Because conditions never matter under Article 1
options.D: Because customs must always use fallback when conditions exist

questions.explanation: Undeterminable conditions can defeat Method 1.

questions.question: Must the methods of customs valuation be applied in sequence?

options.A: Yes, the methods are applied sequentially
options.B: Only methods 1 to 3 must be followed in order

tests.title: Customs Valuation I - Main Test 8
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Customs Valuation I notes.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

options.A: Any service provided by customs

options.C: Only post-import repair services

options.A: It is treated as a royalty

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why is customs value important?

options.A: Because duties and taxes, especially ad valorem duties, are calculated using customs value as the assessment base
options.B: Because customs value replaces classification
options.C: Because customs value determines nationality of the importer
options.D: Because customs value only matters for warehousing

questions.explanation: Ad valorem duties depend on customs value.

questions.question: What does packing cost include?

options.A: Only post-importation repacking
options.B: Only outer wrapping
options.C: The cost of packing materials and packing labor
options.D: Only inland freight

questions.explanation: Packing includes materials and labor.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB means insurance is always included
options.B: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
options.C: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.D: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? When can royalties and licence fees be added to customs value?

options.A: Only when the goods are sold after importation
options.B: Whenever any royalty exists anywhere in the company
options.C: Only if the importer is related to the seller
options.D: When they relate to the goods being valued, are paid as a condition of sale, and are not already included in the price

questions.explanation: These are the three core royalty tests.

questions.question: What is Article 8 mainly about?

options.A: Adjustments to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value
options.B: The sequence of methods 2 to 6 only
options.C: The appeal procedure only
options.D: The legal definition of sale only

questions.explanation: Article 8 governs additions to the price.

questions.question: An importer pays its overseas sourcing representative to find suppliers and negotiate prices. What is the likely treatment if this is a true buying commission?

options.A: It is automatically a proceed of resale
options.B: It must always be added
options.C: It is generally excluded from customs value
options.D: It is treated as a royalty

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are excluded when genuinely paid to the buyer’s own agent.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why are Incoterms or terms of delivery important in valuation documents?

options.A: Because they set customs appeal rights
options.B: Because they determine the importer’s nationality
options.C: Because they replace Article 8
options.D: Because they help show who bears freight, insurance, handling, and related costs and whether those amounts are already in the invoice price

questions.explanation: Incoterms help interpret the price structure.

questions.question: What is Method 6 of customs valuation?

options.A: The fallback method
options.B: The highest-of-two-values method
options.C: The minimum value method
options.D: The domestic market price method

questions.explanation: Method 6 is fallback.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A company imports goods from its foreign parent company. Customs discovers they are related parties. What is the most important question before rejecting Method 1?

options.A: Whether the importer used a buying agent
options.B: Whether the invoice was discounted
options.C: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.D: Whether the goods moved by sea

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key issue is influence on price.

questions.question: An importer supplies free molds to the foreign manufacturer for production of the imported goods. The value is not already in the invoice. What is this most likely to be?

options.A: A buying commission
options.B: An assist that may need to be added under Article 8
options.C: A domestic tax deduction
options.D: A post-importation service

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: Give the full recap formula for transaction value.

options.A: Transaction value equals production cost plus profit in all cases
options.B: Transaction value equals the highest value customs can justify
options.C: Transaction value equals the price actually paid or payable for the goods when sold for export to the country of importation plus only those additions permitted under Article 8, subject to Article 1 conditions
options.D: Transaction value equals domestic price plus all possible costs

questions.explanation: This recap captures the core Method 1 formula.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why are Incoterms or terms of delivery important in valuation documents?

options.A: Because they help show who bears freight, insurance, handling, and related costs and whether those amounts are already in the invoice price
options.B: Because they replace Article 8
options.C: Because they determine the importer’s nationality
options.D: Because they set customs appeal rights

questions.explanation: Incoterms help interpret the price structure.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is a document in the customs valuation lesson on documentation?

options.A: Only a transport document
options.B: Only a printed customs declaration
options.C: Only a paper invoice signed by customs
options.D: Presentation of data in digital or any other form for the purpose of exchanging information and communication

questions.explanation: The lesson uses a broad meaning of document.

questions.question: What optional adjustment items appear under Article 8.2 in these notes?

options.A: Only customs broker fees
options.B: Transport costs to the port or place of importation, loading/unloading and handling charges, and insurance costs
options.C: Domestic taxes, post-import transport, and advertising
options.D: Buying commissions, dividends, and warranties

questions.explanation: These are the optional adjustment items mentioned.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? How are warranty costs treated when already included in the selling price of imported goods?

options.A: They form part of the customs value because they are part of the price actually paid or payable
options.B: They are treated as post-import costs automatically
options.C: They are ignored completely
options.D: They are always deducted

questions.explanation: If already included in the price, they remain part of it.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What stockholding threshold is used in one related-party test?

options.A: Fifty percent
options.B: One percent
options.C: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person
options.D: Ten percent

questions.explanation: The notes mention a five-percent threshold.

questions.question: Does the mere fact that buyer and seller are related automatically make transaction value unacceptable?

options.A: Only if a buying commission is involved
options.B: No
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only if the price is discounted

questions.explanation: Relationship alone is not enough.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What are the key conditions for an Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The cost must always be estimated by customs
options.B: The seller must request the addition
options.C: The goods must be identical goods
options.D: The criteria must be met, the cost incurred by the buyer, not already included in the price, and based on objective and quantifiable data

questions.explanation: These are the general rules for adjustments.

questions.question: Scenario variant: After importation, part of the importer’s resale proceeds must be remitted back to the foreign seller under the contract. What customs valuation issue arises?

options.A: A buying commission issue
options.B: A proceeds-of-subsequent-resale issue under Article 1.1(c) and Article 8.1(d)
options.C: A geographical resale restriction issue only
options.D: An importer appeal issue only

questions.explanation: Resale proceeds returning to the seller may require analysis and adjustment.

questions.question: Can customs reject transaction value just because the price is lower than prevailing market prices?

options.A: Only when goods are imported by road
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Yes, if customs prefers a higher value
options.D: No

questions.explanation: A low price alone is not enough.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays its overseas sourcing representative to find suppliers and negotiate prices. What is the likely treatment if this is a true buying commission?

options.A: It must always be added
options.B: It is automatically a proceed of resale
options.C: It is generally excluded from customs value
options.D: It is treated as a royalty

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are excluded when genuinely paid to the buyer’s own agent.

questions.question: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
options.B: Because FOB means insurance is always included
options.C: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated
options.D: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: To require all Members to use fixed benchmark prices
options.B: To maximize customs revenue at any cost
options.C: To create a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing imported goods and prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values
options.D: To replace all importer appeal rights

questions.explanation: This is the central objective of the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Can transport after importation be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished?

options.A: Only if there is no commercial invoice
options.B: No
options.C: Only in related-party sales
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Post-import transport can be excluded if clearly distinguished.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? If goods are supplied on loan and remain the exporter’s property, is there a sale?

options.A: Yes
options.B: No
options.C: Only if the buyer insures the goods
options.D: Only if the goods are identical goods

questions.explanation: A loan without transfer of ownership is not a sale.

questions.question: A proposed addition is mentioned by the customs officer, but there is no objective and quantifiable data to support the amount. Can the addition be made under Method 1?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes, if the shipment is split
options.C: Yes, customs may estimate it freely
options.D: Yes, if the importer is related

questions.explanation: Without objective and quantifiable data, the adjustment cannot be made.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer supplies free molds to the foreign manufacturer for production of the imported goods. The value is not already in the invoice. What is this most likely to be?

options.A: A domestic tax deduction
options.B: A post-importation service
options.C: A buying commission
options.D: An assist that may need to be added under Article 8

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Article 8 mainly about?

options.A: The appeal procedure only
options.B: The sequence of methods 2 to 6 only
options.C: Adjustments to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value
options.D: The legal definition of sale only

questions.explanation: Article 8 governs additions to the price.

questions.question: Name another category of assists.

options.A: Buying commissions
options.B: Tools, dies, moulds, and similar items used in production of the imported goods
options.C: Post-import warehousing
options.D: Administrative penalties

questions.explanation: Production tools can be assists.

questions.question: The buyer undertakes advertising in the importing country on its own account, and it is not a condition of sale. Does this automatically bar transaction value?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes, because it is always an assist
options.C: Yes, every marketing activity bars Method 1
options.D: Only if the buyer is unrelated

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities do not automatically prevent Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 1.1(c) deal with?

options.A: The importer’s right of appeal
options.B: Proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of imported goods that accrue directly or indirectly to the seller
options.C: Currency conversion
options.D: Confidentiality of customs data

questions.explanation: Article 1.1(c) addresses resale proceeds going back to the seller.

questions.question: A company imports goods from its foreign parent company. Customs discovers they are related parties. What is the most important question before rejecting Method 1?

options.A: Whether the invoice was discounted
options.B: Whether the goods moved by sea
options.C: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.D: Whether the importer used a buying agent

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key issue is influence on price.

tests.title: Customs Valuation I - Main Test 11
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Customs Valuation I notes.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: What does Part I of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement cover?

options.A: Rules on customs valuation in Articles 1 to 17
options.B: Administration and dispute settlement only
options.C: Only final provisions
options.D: Only technical committee rules

questions.explanation: Part I contains the valuation rules.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is Method 5 of customs valuation?

options.A: Transaction value of identical goods
options.B: Deductive value
options.C: Computed value
options.D: Fallback method

questions.explanation: Method 5 is computed value.

questions.question: Customs wants to verify whether the invoice price already includes insurance and freight up to the place of importation. Which type of information is most directly helpful?

options.A: The number of customs officers involved
options.B: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents
options.C: The importer’s domestic advertising budget
options.D: The importer’s family relationship only

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: Why does the Agreement prefer transaction value?

options.A: Because it always produces the highest duty
options.B: Because it can be used even when there is no sale
options.C: Because it ignores the price actually paid
options.D: Because it is based on the actual commercial transaction and reflects the real value of the imported goods as closely as possible

questions.explanation: Transaction value best reflects commercial reality.

questions.question: An importer pays its overseas sourcing representative to find suppliers and negotiate prices. What is the likely treatment if this is a true buying commission?

options.A: It is generally excluded from customs value
options.B: It must always be added
options.C: It is automatically a proceed of resale
options.D: It is treated as a royalty

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are excluded when genuinely paid to the buyer’s own agent.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the third category of allowable restriction under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Restrictions based on domestic market prices
options.B: Restrictions imposed by a parent company automatically
options.C: Restrictions that increase customs revenue
options.D: Restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods

questions.explanation: Insignificant restrictions do not bar Method 1.

questions.question: What is Method 1 of customs valuation?

options.A: Fallback method
options.B: Transaction value of identical goods
options.C: Transaction value
options.D: Computed value

questions.explanation: Method 1 is transaction value.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.B: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
options.C: Because FOB means insurance is always included
options.D: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one type of legal deduction that may be excluded if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable.

options.A: Assists supplied by the buyer
options.B: Containers and packing
options.C: Commissions to the seller’s agent
options.D: Charges for construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance undertaken after importation

questions.explanation: Post-importation service charges may be excluded if properly distinguished.

questions.question: Must the seller be in the exporting country for transaction value to apply?

options.A: Only when royalties are payable
options.B: No, the key issue is whether there was a sale for export to the country of importation
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: Only if the buyer is unrelated

questions.explanation: Seller location is not the deciding factor.

questions.question: An importer supplies free molds to the foreign manufacturer for production of the imported goods. The value is not already in the invoice. What is this most likely to be?

options.A: A domestic tax deduction
options.B: A buying commission
options.C: An assist that may need to be added under Article 8
options.D: A post-importation service

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why may branch office transfers fail to qualify as sales?

options.A: Because the branch office and parent company may not be separate legal entities, so there is no sale between separate persons
options.B: Because customs forbids related-party trade
options.C: Because branches never import goods
options.D: Because they always use fallback method

questions.explanation: A transfer within the same legal person is not a sale.

questions.question: An importer pays its overseas sourcing representative to find suppliers and negotiate prices. What is the likely treatment if this is a true buying commission?

options.A: It is treated as a royalty
options.B: It must always be added
options.C: It is generally excluded from customs value
options.D: It is automatically a proceed of resale

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are excluded when genuinely paid to the buyer’s own agent.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What stockholding threshold is used in one related-party test?

options.A: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock of both buyer and seller held by a third person
options.B: One percent
options.C: Fifty percent
options.D: Ten percent

questions.explanation: The notes mention a five-percent threshold.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one category of assists under Article 8.1(b).

options.A: Post-import transport
options.B: Materials, components, parts, and similar items incorporated in the imported goods
options.C: Buyer’s domestic advertising
options.D: Import-country duties and taxes

questions.explanation: This is one assist category.

questions.question: How are warranty costs treated when already included in the selling price of imported goods?

options.A: They are always deducted
options.B: They are ignored completely
options.C: They are treated as post-import costs automatically
options.D: They form part of the customs value because they are part of the price actually paid or payable

questions.explanation: If already included in the price, they remain part of it.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? If goods are sold CIF, what does that generally imply for customs valuation?

options.A: No transport-related costs are included
options.B: These costs are always excluded
options.C: Freight, insurance, and related charges to the place of importation are generally already included in the price
options.D: Only freight is included

questions.explanation: CIF usually includes cost, insurance, and freight.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer pays a trademark royalty tied to the imported goods, and the seller requires that payment as a condition of sale. The royalty is not already in the invoice. What is the likely result?

options.A: The royalty may need to be added to customs value
options.B: It must always be excluded
options.C: It is irrelevant because trademarks never matter
options.D: It becomes a post-import deduction

questions.explanation: Royalties may be dutiable if they meet the Article 8 tests.

questions.question: Can customs reject transaction value just because the price is lower than prevailing market prices?

options.A: Yes, if customs prefers a higher value
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Only when goods are imported by road
options.D: No

questions.explanation: A low price alone is not enough.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A post-importation cost deduction
options.B: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.C: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing
options.D: A permitted geographical resale restriction

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing

options.C: A post-importation cost deduction
options.D: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it

options.D: Customs must apply minimum values

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why are transport terms like CIF and FOB important in customs valuation?

options.A: Because they determine whether a sale exists
options.B: Because they determine the HS code
options.C: Because they help show whether freight, insurance, and related costs are already included in the invoice price or must be added
options.D: Because they replace the need for a bill of lading

questions.explanation: Incoterms help interpret the price structure.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can the buyer’s own marketing activities form part of customs value?

options.A: Yes, if done after importation
options.B: Normally no, if undertaken on the buyer’s own account and not as a condition of sale
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: Only if a buying commission is paid

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities may fall outside customs value if not a condition of sale.

questions.question: What optional adjustment items appear under Article 8.2 in these notes?

options.A: Only customs broker fees

options.C: Domestic taxes, post-import transport, and advertising
options.D: Transport costs to the port or place of importation, loading/unloading and handling charges, and insurance costs

questions.explanation: These are the optional adjustment items mentioned.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is customs value in simple terms?

options.A: The transport cost after importation only
options.B: The value declared or determined for imported goods for customs purposes and used as the basis for assessing duties and taxes
options.C: The amount of profit made by the importer
options.D: The domestic retail price of locally made goods

questions.explanation: The notes define customs value as the basis for assessing duties and taxes on imported goods.

questions.question: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods

options.A: The domestic retail price of locally made goods

options.D: The transport cost after importation only

options.A: Only when goods are imported by road
options.B: Yes, if customs prefers a higher value
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: No

options.A: It is generally excluded from customs value
options.B: It is automatically a proceed of resale
options.C: It must always be added
options.D: It is treated as a royalty

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are excluded when genuinely paid to the buyer’s own agent.

questions.question: Which methods may sometimes be reversed at the importer’s request?

options.A: Identical goods and similar goods

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer undertakes advertising in the importing country on its own account, and it is not a condition of sale. Does this automatically bar transaction value?

options.A: Yes, every marketing activity bars Method 1

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities do not automatically prevent Method 1.

options.A: No
options.B: Only in related-party sales
options.C: Yes

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key issue is influence on price.

questions.question: Goods are shipped by a foreign supplier to a Kenyan distributor on consignment, with ownership remaining abroad until local resale. Can Article 1 transaction value normally be used at importation?

questions.question: What is Method 3 of customs valuation?

options.A: Computed value
options.B: Transaction value of similar goods

questions.question: Scenario variant: A company imports goods from its foreign parent company. Customs discovers they are related parties. What is the most important question before rejecting Method 1?

options.B: The importer’s domestic advertising budget
options.C: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents
options.D: The importer’s family relationship only

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is Method 3 of customs valuation?

options.A: Fallback method
options.B: Transaction value of similar goods
options.C: Deductive value
options.D: Computed value

questions.explanation: Method 3 is similar goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one typical function of a buying agent.

options.A: Finding suppliers of the goods wanted by the buyer
options.B: Setting tariff rates
options.C: Issuing bills of lading
options.D: Assessing customs duty

questions.explanation: A buying agent helps the importer source goods.

questions.question: A proposed addition is mentioned by the customs officer, but there is no objective and quantifiable data to support the amount. Can the addition be made under Method 1?

options.A: Yes, customs may estimate it freely
options.B: Yes, if the importer is related
options.C: No
options.D: Yes, if the shipment is split

questions.explanation: Without objective and quantifiable data, the adjustment cannot be made.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Why is greater uniformity and certainty important in customs valuation?

options.A: Because customs officers should have unlimited discretion
options.B: Because every importer must pay the same amount
options.C: Because valuation should vary by port
options.D: Because traders should know the rules in advance and trust that valuation will be applied consistently and fairly

questions.explanation: Uniformity and certainty support predictable trade.

tests.title: Customs Valuation I - Main Test 12
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Customs Valuation I notes.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Goods are shipped by a foreign supplier to a Kenyan distributor on consignment, with ownership remaining abroad until local resale. Can Article 1 transaction value normally be used at importation?

options.A: Usually no, because there may be no sale for export at the time of importation
options.B: Yes, if a packing list is available
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: Only if the goods are insured

questions.explanation: Consignment often means no completed sale at the moment of importation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the first key test for adding a royalty or licence fee?

options.A: It must be described as a dividend
options.B: It must be unrelated to the sale
options.C: It must be paid after importation
options.D: It must relate to the imported goods

questions.explanation: Relation to the imported goods is the first test.

questions.question: What is customs value in simple terms?

options.A: The amount of profit made by the importer
options.B: The domestic retail price of locally made goods
options.C: The value declared or determined for imported goods for customs purposes and used as the basis for assessing duties and taxes
options.D: The transport cost after importation only

questions.explanation: The notes define customs value as the basis for assessing duties and taxes on imported goods.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What is another way to show no price influence in a related-party sale?

options.A: Show that the buyer paid late
options.B: Show that the price is the same as in sales to unrelated buyers in comparable circumstances
options.C: Show that the goods moved by road
options.D: Show that the importer used a letter of credit

questions.explanation: Comparable sales to unrelated buyers can support the price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one way to show that a related-party price was not influenced by the relationship?

options.A: Show that transport costs are high
options.B: Show that the invoice is handwritten
options.C: Show that the price is consistent with normal industry pricing practice
options.D: Show that duties were already paid

questions.explanation: Industry practice can support acceptability.

questions.question: An importer pays its overseas sourcing representative to find suppliers and negotiate prices. What is the likely treatment if this is a true buying commission?

options.A: It must always be added
options.B: It is treated as a royalty
options.C: It is automatically a proceed of resale
options.D: It is generally excluded from customs value

questions.explanation: Buying commissions are excluded when genuinely paid to the buyer’s own agent.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Name one category of related persons under Article 15.

options.A: Members of the same family
options.B: Any two importers in the same port
options.C: Any seller and carrier
options.D: Any broker and customs officer

questions.explanation: Family members are one related-party category.

questions.question: Customs wants to verify whether the invoice price already includes insurance and freight up to the place of importation. Which type of information is most directly helpful?

options.A: The importer’s domestic advertising budget
options.B: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents
options.C: The number of customs officers involved
options.D: The importer’s family relationship only

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer undertakes advertising in the importing country on its own account, and it is not a condition of sale. Does this automatically bar transaction value?

options.A: Yes, every marketing activity bars Method 1
options.B: Yes, because it is always an assist
options.C: No
options.D: Only if the buyer is unrelated

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities do not automatically prevent Method 1.

questions.question: The buyer undertakes advertising in the importing country on its own account, and it is not a condition of sale. Does this automatically bar transaction value?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if the buyer is unrelated
options.C: Yes, because it is always an assist
options.D: Yes, every marketing activity bars Method 1

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities do not automatically prevent Method 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What happens if there is no quantifiable data for a proposed Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: Customs must estimate it upward
options.B: The goods must be reclassified
options.C: The importer must accept a minimum value
options.D: The adjustment cannot be made under transaction value

questions.explanation: Without quantifiable data, the addition cannot be made.

questions.question: What are the two broad ways of examining related-party transaction value?

options.A: By applying Article 8 directly without inquiry
options.B: By observing the circumstances surrounding the sale or by measuring against test values
options.C: By comparing only with transport costs
options.D: By using minimum values or domestic prices

questions.explanation: These are the two accepted approaches.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Do hire or leasing contracts normally constitute sales under the Agreement?

options.A: Only if they last more than one year
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Only if customs requests it
options.D: No, leasing contracts are generally for rent or lease and not a sale

questions.explanation: Leasing is generally not a sale.

questions.question: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.B: A post-importation cost deduction
options.C: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing
options.D: A permitted geographical resale restriction

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: Why can a purchase order matter in customs valuation?

options.A: Because it is the same as a bill of lading
options.B: Because it helps confirm the agreed goods, quantities, terms, and sometimes the intended method of ordering or pricing
options.C: Because it proves insurance risks only
options.D: Because it is a transport title document

questions.explanation: A purchase order helps explain the transaction background.

questions.question: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: Customs must ignore the supporting documents
options.B: Customs must reject Method 1 because the price is low
options.C: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it
options.D: Customs must apply minimum values

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? If goods are sold FOB, what must customs often verify?

options.A: Whether freight, insurance, and related charges to the place of importation are not included and therefore need treatment under Article 8.2
options.B: Whether the importer owns the vessel
options.C: Whether customs value must always be rejected
options.D: Whether a buying commission becomes dutiable

questions.explanation: FOB often excludes main carriage and insurance.

questions.question: Why are documents important in customs valuation?

options.A: They are relevant only after customs has fixed the value
options.B: They matter only for packaging decisions
options.C: They replace the need for Article 1 analysis
options.D: They support clearance, origin identification, statistics, audit, intelligence, dispute settlement, revenue collection, and proof of ownership

questions.explanation: Documents support many customs functions including valuation.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated
options.B: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.C: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2
options.D: Because FOB means insurance is always included

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Give the full recap formula for transaction value.

options.A: Transaction value equals domestic price plus all possible costs
options.B: Transaction value equals the price actually paid or payable for the goods when sold for export to the country of importation plus only those additions permitted under Article 8, subject to Article 1 conditions
options.C: Transaction value equals production cost plus profit in all cases
options.D: Transaction value equals the highest value customs can justify

questions.explanation: This recap captures the core Method 1 formula.

questions.question: What four questions help test whether transaction value is acceptable under Article 1.1?

options.A: Whether the importer has an appeal right, a broker, a bank, and an agent
options.B: Whether the goods are insured, packaged, labeled, and warehoused
options.C: Whether there are restrictions on disposition or use, undeterminable conditions, proceeds accruing to the seller, and whether buyer and seller are related
options.D: Whether the goods are perishable, bulky, identical, or exempt

questions.explanation: These are the four major Article 1.1 tests.

questions.question: Customs wants to verify whether the invoice price already includes insurance and freight up to the place of importation. Which type of information is most directly helpful?

options.A: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents
options.B: The importer’s domestic advertising budget
options.C: The number of customs officers involved
options.D: The importer’s family relationship only

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 1.1(c) deal with?

options.A: Currency conversion
options.B: The importer’s right of appeal
options.C: Confidentiality of customs data
options.D: Proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of imported goods that accrue directly or indirectly to the seller

questions.explanation: Article 1.1(c) addresses resale proceeds going back to the seller.

questions.question: Customs wants to verify whether the invoice price already includes insurance and freight up to the place of importation. Which type of information is most directly helpful?

options.A: The number of customs officers involved
options.B: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents
options.C: The importer’s family relationship only
options.D: The importer’s domestic advertising budget

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Name one category of assists under Article 8.1(b).

options.A: Materials, components, parts, and similar items incorporated in the imported goods
options.B: Buyer’s domestic advertising
options.C: Post-import transport
options.D: Import-country duties and taxes

questions.explanation: This is one assist category.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What happens if there is no quantifiable data for a proposed Article 8 adjustment?

options.A: The importer must accept a minimum value
options.B: Customs must estimate it upward
options.C: The goods must be reclassified
options.D: The adjustment cannot be made under transaction value

questions.explanation: Without quantifiable data, the addition cannot be made.

questions.question: Which methods may sometimes be reversed at the importer’s request?

options.A: Identical goods and similar goods
options.B: Transaction value and identical goods
options.C: Deductive value and computed value, meaning Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed in order
options.D: Computed value and fallback

questions.explanation: Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed on request.

questions.question: A company imports goods from its foreign parent company. Customs discovers they are related parties. What is the most important question before rejecting Method 1?

options.A: Whether the invoice was discounted
options.B: Whether the relationship influenced the price
options.C: Whether the importer used a buying agent
options.D: Whether the goods moved by sea

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone is not enough; the key issue is influence on price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the three key elements for applying transaction value according to the notes?

options.A: The duty amount, warehousing period, and bond
options.B: The transport mode, insurance, and customs office
options.C: The tariff rate, the origin mark, and the HS code
options.D: The sale, the sale for export to the country of importation, and the price actually paid or payable

questions.explanation: These are the three core elements.

questions.question: If goods are supplied on loan and remain the exporter’s property, is there a sale?

options.A: Only if the buyer insures the goods
options.B: Yes
options.C: No
options.D: Only if the goods are identical goods

questions.explanation: A loan without transfer of ownership is not a sale.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs wants to verify whether the invoice price already includes insurance and freight up to the place of importation. Which type of information is most directly helpful?

options.A: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents
options.B: The number of customs officers involved
options.C: The importer’s domestic advertising budget
options.D: The importer’s family relationship only

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Why are documents important in customs valuation?

options.A: They replace the need for Article 1 analysis
options.B: They support clearance, origin identification, statistics, audit, intelligence, dispute settlement, revenue collection, and proof of ownership
options.C: They are relevant only after customs has fixed the value
options.D: They matter only for packaging decisions

questions.explanation: Documents support many customs functions including valuation.

questions.question: Does the mere fact that buyer and seller are related automatically make transaction value unacceptable?

options.A: Only if a buying commission is involved
options.B: Only if the price is discounted
options.C: No
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Relationship alone is not enough.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Name one category of related persons under Article 15.

options.A: Members of the same family
options.B: Any two importers in the same port
options.C: Any broker and customs officer
options.D: Any seller and carrier

questions.explanation: Family members are one related-party category.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice is on FOB terms. Customs needs to know whether freight and insurance to the port of importation should be considered. Why does FOB matter here?

options.A: Because FOB means insurance is always included
options.B: Because FOB removes the need for a bill of lading
options.C: Because FOB proves the parties are unrelated
options.D: Because FOB may mean those costs are not included in the invoice price and may need treatment under Article 8.2

questions.explanation: FOB helps customs determine whether transport-related costs are included.

questions.question: What is a document check?

options.A: A post-import warehouse audit only
options.B: A systematic analysis of all supporting documents to a customs declaration to facilitate proper valuation of imported goods
options.C: A physical inspection of the goods only
options.D: A customs court hearing

questions.explanation: Document check is a systematic review of supporting papers and data.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Goods are shipped by a foreign supplier to a Kenyan distributor on consignment, with ownership remaining abroad until local resale. Can Article 1 transaction value normally be used at importation?

options.A: Usually no, because there may be no sale for export at the time of importation
options.B: Yes, if a packing list is available
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: Only if the goods are insured

questions.explanation: Consignment often means no completed sale at the moment of importation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How should the value of an assist be apportioned?

options.A: Only over the first shipment
options.B: Always equally over all future imports regardless of evidence
options.C: By customs estimate alone
options.D: In a reasonable manner appropriate to the circumstances and supported by documentation

questions.explanation: Assist allocation must be reasonable and documented.

questions.question: If goods are sold CIF, what does that generally imply for customs valuation?

options.A: These costs are always excluded
options.B: Freight, insurance, and related charges to the place of importation are generally already included in the price
options.C: No transport-related costs are included
options.D: Only freight is included

questions.explanation: CIF usually includes cost, insurance, and freight.

questions.question: A proposed addition is mentioned by the customs officer, but there is no objective and quantifiable data to support the amount. Can the addition be made under Method 1?

options.A: Yes, if the importer is related
options.B: Yes, if the shipment is split
options.C: No
options.D: Yes, customs may estimate it freely

questions.explanation: Without objective and quantifiable data, the adjustment cannot be made.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs wants to verify whether the invoice price already includes insurance and freight up to the place of importation. Which type of information is most directly helpful?

options.A: The number of customs officers involved
options.B: The importer’s family relationship only
options.C: The importer’s domestic advertising budget
options.D: The Incoterms or delivery terms shown in the commercial documents

questions.explanation: Delivery terms like FOB or CIF help show what is included in the price.

questions.question: What is Method 6 of customs valuation?

options.A: The minimum value method
options.B: The fallback method
options.C: The domestic market price method
options.D: The highest-of-two-values method

questions.explanation: Method 6 is fallback.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? Which methods may sometimes be reversed at the importer’s request?

options.A: Deductive value and computed value, meaning Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed in order
options.B: Transaction value and identical goods
options.C: Identical goods and similar goods
options.D: Computed value and fallback

questions.explanation: Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed on request.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What optional adjustment items appear under Article 8.2 in these notes?

options.A: Only customs broker fees
options.B: Buying commissions, dividends, and warranties
options.C: Transport costs to the port or place of importation, loading/unloading and handling charges, and insurance costs
options.D: Domestic taxes, post-import transport, and advertising

questions.explanation: These are the optional adjustment items mentioned.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A seller agrees to sell imported goods only if the buyer also purchases unrelated goods in fixed quantities. What potential Article 1 issue does this raise?

options.A: A permitted geographical resale restriction
options.B: A condition or consideration whose value may not be determinable with respect to the imported goods
options.C: A post-importation cost deduction
options.D: An automatic proof of arm’s-length pricing

questions.explanation: Linked-purchase conditions may prevent use of transaction value.

questions.question: The commercial invoice shows a low price, but the purchase order, contract, and payment records all support it as a valid bulk discount. What should customs understand from this?

options.A: Customs must apply minimum values
options.B: Customs must ignore the supporting documents
options.C: Customs must reject Method 1 because the price is low
options.D: A low price alone does not invalidate transaction value if the supporting documents credibly explain it

questions.explanation: The notes stress that low price alone is not enough for rejection.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does packing cost include?

options.A: Only post-importation repacking
options.B: Only outer wrapping
options.C: The cost of packing materials and packing labor
options.D: Only inland freight

questions.explanation: Packing includes materials and labor.

questions.question: Are dividends paid by the buyer to the seller always part of the customs value?

options.A: No, not if they do not relate to the imported goods
options.B: Yes, whenever the buyer and seller are related
options.C: Yes, if they are paid after importation
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: Dividends unrelated to the goods are not part of customs value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the notes? What does Part II of the Agreement cover?

options.A: Only special and differential treatment
options.B: Only definitions and annexes
options.C: Valuation methods only
options.D: Administration, consultations, and dispute settlement in Articles 18 and 19

questions.explanation: Part II addresses administration and disputes.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does packing cost include?

options.A: Only inland freight
options.B: Only outer wrapping
options.C: The cost of packing materials and packing labor
options.D: Only post-importation repacking

questions.explanation: Packing includes materials and labor.
